Import the destination files

For convenience(?) the following code block will output a URL where you can import your destination files.

Personalize BTPGLOBALACCOUNT below to equal the name of your BTP Global Account.., then run it..:

 

export BTPGLOBALACCOUNT=20250102b
btp logout
btp login --sso --url https://canary.cli.btp.int.sap --subdomain $BTPGLOBALACCOUNT
export BTP_GLOBAL_ACCOUNT_ID=`btp get accounts/global-account --global-account $BTPGLOBALACCOUNT | grep "global account id" | awk '{print $4;}'` 
export BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID=`btp list accounts/subaccount | grep EU12 | awk '{print $1;}'`
echo "BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID": $BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID
echo "Open this URL to import your destination files: https://canary.cockpit.btp.int.sap/cockpit/#/globalaccount/$BTP_GLOBAL_ACCOUNT_ID/subaccount/$BTP_EU12_SUBACCOUNT_ID/destinations"
echo "and import the destination files $(pwd)/destination*.txt"

 

This will print the URL of the site you should now open to import your destination files.. Open that URL and import and save your 2 new destination files there:

Compile and deploy the Joule Capabilities

When you compile an application, specify which scenarios it will have:

 

cat << EOF > ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/da.sapdas.yaml
schema_version: 1.0.0
name: airwarmupjoulewithdocker
capabilities:
  - type: local
    folder: ./helloworld
  - type: local
    folder: ./weather
  - type: local
    folder: ./products
  - type: local
    folder: ./employees
EOF

 

We can now deploy our Joule code:

 

joule delete aiwarmupwithjoule
cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities
joule deploy -c -n "aiwarmupwithjoule"

 

Confirm deployment

Use the joule list command to list your deployments:

 

joule list

 

which should show 

 

Digital Assistant Name ID
aiwarmupwithjoule      xxxx

 

Testing your Joule app

Add tests for the weather capability:

 

mkdir -p ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/weather/tests/features

cat << EOF > ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/weather/tests/features/example.feature
@weather
Feature: Weather capability with GenAI
    Feature Description    
    Background: Initial state
        Given I log in
        And I start a new conversation   
    Scenario: Munich Weather
        When I say "How is the weather"
        Then response has 1 message
        And message type at index 0 is text
        And first message content contains "Could you please provide the name of the city"
        When I say "Munich"
        Then response has 1 messages
        And response relates to "weather in Munich"
EOF

 

And try it out:

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/weather
joule test aiwarmupwithjoule

 

You should see something similar to:

See https://wiki.one.int.sap/wiki/display/das/Test+the+Capability for tips and tricks on testing..

Launch Joule

 

joule launch aiwarmupwithjoule

 

This should open the Joule GUI where you can try out commands like:

  • what capabilities do you have
  • what is the weather like in Berlin

Activate Debug Mode

  • It is helpful to peak under the covers. 
  • Widen the UI (to be sure all buttons are shown) and you should then see a debug button in the joule client window
  • Click the debug button to open and explore the paths each joule query takes

Try out some Joule queries

For example:

  • what is the weather like in Berlin
  • show me the products
  • who are my employees
    • Note that this returns blank/broken images of the employees.
    • Trace through the code to figure out why (see the file get_employees.yaml for tips..)

    • We will fix that with a Spring Endpoint 🙂

Part 3: Extend an existing Capability with a new Spring Endpoint

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/
git clone https://github.tools.sap/D061192/aiwarmupspringendpoint.git

 

  • Build the Spring Endpoint:

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/aiwarmupspringendpoint
mvn clean install

 

Confirm the spring endpoint works locally..

 

java -jar target/demo-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar

 

  • Ping the endpoint at
    • localhost:8080 and
    • localhost:8080/get-file?file=1.png
    • and confirm you see valid responses
  • Then ctrl-c out

Push the Spring Endpoint to your Cloud Foundry on BTP

Sign in with the default provider:

 

cf login -a https://api.cf.eu12.hana.ondemand.com --sso

 

Push your Spring endpoint to cloud foundry..

 

cf create-space aiwarmup
cf target -s aiwarmup
cf push

 

Run this block and make the 3 replacements that it outputs:

 

export CF_APP_ENDPOINT=https://`cf apps | grep aiwarmupimages  | awk '{print $5;}'`
echo $CF_APP_ENDPOINT
echo "Modify the 1st image endpoint in ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/employees/functions/get_employees.yaml to $CF_APP_ENDPOINT/get-file?file=1.png"
echo "Modify the 2nd image endpoint in ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/employees/functions/get_employees.yaml to $CF_APP_ENDPOINT/get-file?file=2.png"
echo "Modify the 3rd image endpoint in ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities/employees/functions/get_employees.yaml to $CF_APP_ENDPOINT/get-file?file=3.png"

 

Redeploy the joule instance

 

cd ~/jouledemo2025/joule-functions-example/capabilities
joule deploy -c -n "aiwarmupwithjoule"

 

Relaunch joule 

 

joule launch aiwarmupwithjoule

 

Restart the Joule conversation to drop any cache:

Try "Tell me about my employees" again, and work through the debug output, and code changes, to see why we now have images appearing.

We collect favourite upskilling material in our crowd-sourced portal at https://enablement2024.a9ab9c7.stage.kyma.ondemand.com/  which you might find helpful.

If you like this sort of blog let me know and I'll post more.

Happy exploring 🙂

entry.title: SuccessFactors Joule - Joule and EC Quick Actions
entry.content:
Joule in combination with Employee Central provides us a huge set of use cases where Joule can support your employees and managers to run EC related processes. [List of transaction use cases]

To get this use cases running it is important to implement EC Quick Actions for the available cases. [Guide]

 

Let me try to show how the configuration of EC Data Model, Quick Action and Joule comes together.

1. Business Configuration: Here you define the fields, rules etc. that are available for employee data in overall and what you can see/change on the employee profile

2. RBP based on Business Configuration: Here you define who can see, edit, change, delete, create what details of whom

3. Configure Quick Action: With the configuration of the quick action you define what fields (from the datamodel/business configuration) are necessary for a dedicated process (e.g. if you have defined a process for working time changes than you can define that standard weekly hours, number of days per week are relevant for the process). The selected template defines the set of the available fields

4. Permissions for Quick Actions: Here you define who can run what quick action for whom (typically it is foreseen for Managers and Employees but it is also possible for HR)

With this 4 configuration steps you are ready to run EC based scenarios on Joule.

 

 

How can you validate if you can run a quick action based scenario on joule?

Let us assume I'm logged in as Carla and I want to change my own working location.

What do I need: 

- an EC Employee Profile (with bio info, job info, person info)

- permissions to run the "change location" quick action for me

 

To check the permission open the profile of Carla and check the available actions.

Because the quick action is available on profile level we should be able to run the scenario in joule also.

 

How does joule knows what information needs to be provided?

Joule is running step by step through the quick action definition.

You have defined what fields are relevant for the quick action. So Joule will ask you for each and every defined field if you want to change or to keep. 

If all the defined fields are provided user will see a summary and can save the data. In case a workflow is defined the workflow get's triggered.

One special hint: On the Quick Action template you can define what event reason is written for the defined action. In case you are NOT using event reason derivation you have to define the event reason on quick action template level.

 

Let us have a look to an example where the scenario on joule would not work.

Here we (again as Carla) see an "empty" profile where we have only an user but no employee data (no employment, no job).

In this case the quick action would not be available in the profile (even if my logged-in user has quick action permissions).

And so Joule will answer with "it is not possible".

 

 

 

 

Typical "issues" during testing:

- Quick Actions not defined

- No permission to run the quick action for the selected user

- selected user has no EC data (most common issue during testing on non-prod systems)

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Standard Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!

entry.title: Revolutionizing Enterprise Intelligence: SAP Business AI, Joule, and Embedded AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a crucial driving force for innovation and growth. SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, offers a suite of AI solutions tailored to meet the diverse needs of modern businesses. Among these, SAP Business AI stands out, with two primary offerings:  Joule and Embedded AI. This blog explores the distinctions between these two powerful solutions, highlighting their unique features and capabilities.

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

What is SAP Business AI?

SAP Business AI represents a collection of intelligent technologies designed to automate and optimize business processes within the enterprise environment. By leveraging machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and other advanced AI techniques, SAP Business AI enables organizations to make data-driven decisions faster.

Picture1: Key features of SAP Business AI

Key Features of SAP Business AI

  1. Embedded AI: SAP embeds AI capabilities directly into its existing solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP C/4HANA, and SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring seamless user experience and quick adoption.
  2. Automated Machine Learning: SAP Business AI leverages automated machine learning to build and train models rapidly, without the need for extensive data science expertise.
  3. Natural Language Processing (NLP): It uses NLP to enable conversational AI, allowing users to interact with SAP systems using natural language.
  4. Robotic Process Automation (RPA): SAP Business AI employs RPA to automate repetitive tasks, freeing up human resources for more strategic work.
  5. Predictive Analytics: It offers predictive analytics to help businesses anticipate future trends, customer behavior, and market changes.

This blog is going to focus on Joule and Embedded AI and how they are used in SAP S/4HANA to improve user experience and make product extensions easier and more efficient.

Understanding Joule

Joule is a platform that allows businesses to develop, deploy, and manage AI applications with ease. It serves as a comprehensive toolbox for AI and Machine Learning (ML), providing developers with the necessary resources to build intelligent solutions tailored to specific business needs. Key features of Joule include:

  1. Flexible Development: Joule offers a versatile environment for developing AI models using preferred tools and frameworks. This flexibility empowers developers to create custom AI solutions that seamlessly integrate with existing systems.
  2. Scalability: The platform supports scalable deployment of AI applications, ensuring that businesses can handle increasing volumes of data and user requests efficiently.
  3. Ecosystem Compatibility: Joule is designed to work well with various SAP and third-party applications, fostering a collaborative ecosystem where different tools and technologies can interoperate.

In the ever-evolving landscape of enterprise resource planning, SAP S/4HANA has emerged as a powerful solution, offering a wide range of functionalities to streamline business processes. However, navigating through its complex interface can sometimes be challenging for users. This is where Joule comes into play. Joule is an innovative tool designed to simplify navigation in SAP S/4HANA, enhancing user experience and productivity.

Joule is a user-friendly navigation tool that helps users quickly find and access the required transactions, reports, and other functionalities within SAP S/4HANA. It acts as a search engine within the SAP environment, making it easier for users to locate specific features without having to memorize transaction codes or navigate through multiple menus.

 Key Features of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (I don’t know if you want to include Private as well – if yes, keep it as SAP S/4HANA Cloud)

  1. Intuitive Search: Joule allows users to perform keyword-based searches. Whether you're looking for a specific transaction code, report, or even a custom SAP program, Joule can find it for you in a matter of seconds.
  2. Smart Suggestions: As you type in the search bar, Joule provides smart suggestions based on your input. This feature significantly reduces the time spent searching for the right option, enhancing overall efficiency.
  3. Personalized Experience: Joule learns from your usage patterns and adapts to your preferences. It keeps track of frequently used transactions and reports, making them easily accessible from the home screen.
  4. Integrated Navigation: Joule integrates seamlessly with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud interface, providing a unified navigation experience. This integration ensures that users can access all necessary functionalities without having to switch between different tools or interfaces.
  5. Accessibility: Joule is accessible from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud home screen, making it convenient for users to start their search right from the main interface. This eliminates the need to open separate applications or modules.

Benefits of Using Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Improved Productivity: With Joule, users can quickly find what they need, reducing the time spent on navigation. This leads to increased productivity and efficiency.
  2. Reduced Training Time: New users can easily adapt to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with Joule's intuitive interface. The tool's simplicity and user-friendliness reduce the learning curve, making training more efficient.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Joule provides a seamless and personalized experience, tailored to each user's needs. This enhances overall satisfaction and makes the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system more approachable.
  4. Better Decision Making: By making it easier to access reports and analytics, Joule helps users make informed decisions promptly. This is particularly beneficial for managers and executives who need quick access to specific data.

Steps to Use Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Access Joule: From the SAP S/4 HANA home screen, locate the Joule search bar. This is usually placed in a prominent position for easy access.
  2. Enter Keywords: Type in the keywords related to the transaction, report, or functionality you are looking for. As you type, Joule will provide relevant suggestions.
  3. Select the Desired Option: From the list of suggestions, select the desired option. Joule will navigate you directly to the relevant screen or transaction.

The screen shot below shows an example of how Joule can be used to quickly find information in your S/4HANA Cloud system. In this case the user wants to know how to create a Sales Order in the system.  Instead of having to navigate through the help menus the user can simply type “How do I create a sales order” in the Joule editor and Joule will display all the information necessary for the user to get started.

 

Figure 2: Sample of navigation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Joule

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

Figure 3: Form before the changes

 

Figure 4: Form after the changes

Conclusion

SAP Business AI offers a powerful suite of tools to help businesses achieve their digital transformation goals. Joule provides a flexible platform for easy navigation and fast data retrieval.  Joule can also be used to develop and deploy custom AI solutions, catering to the needs of developers and data scientists. In contrast, Embedded AI focuses on delivering ready-to-use AI functionalities integrated within existing SAP applications, providing immediate value to end-users.

Joule and Embedded AI can be used concurrently to simplify the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system by allowing users to retrieve data and perform operations faster and more efficiently.

For organizations looking to build custom AI applications, Joule also offers unparalleled flexibility and scalability. On the other hand, Embedded AI is ideal for businesses seeking quick, out-of-the-box AI solutions to enhance their existing SAP environments.

Ultimately, both Joule and Embedded AI play crucial roles in the SAP Business AI ecosystem, empowering businesses to unlock the full potential of artificial intelligence and drive sustainable growth.

Stay tuned for more insights into the evolving world of enterprise AI and its transformative impact on modern business operations.

entry.title: The Relevance of Business Applications in the Agentic Future of AI
entry.content:
Introduction

 

Hello everyone. I want to dive into something I am quite excited about: the whole debate around AI “agents” and how they might (or might not) replace the traditional business app layer. If you’ve caught Matthew Berman’s YouTube breakdown of Satya Nadella’s recent comments, you know it sparked some confusion—like “SaaS is dead,” and “we don’t need ERPs anymore.” Seriously? 

Microsoft CEO’s Shocking Prediction: “Agents Will Replace ALL Software"

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uGOLYz2pgr8&t=2s 

Let’s calm down, it may have gotten out of hand, let’s talk it through.

Because here’s the thing: yes, AI agents are going to change a lot. They’ll unify logic, be the new UX, handle multi-repo CRUD, and talk to a dozen backends at once. But that doesn’t mean the last 50 years of SAP’s collective business intelligence—its “special sauce”—is going to vanish. In fact, SAP’s entire mission to build enterprise-ready GenAI (like Joule) is based on leveraging that existing IP, not tossing it out the window. Let’s see why.

 

1 - The Rise of the AI Agent

 

Agents as the New Logic Layer

 

Satya Nadella’s main point is that AI agents will become the central logic tier, orchestrating everything from finance to supply chain. Instead of flipping between multiple apps—think SAP S/4HANA for finance, plus a separate CRM for leads, plus another tool for HR—AI agents promise a single place for these tasks. You say, “Hey agent, update my sales forecast and check inventory,” and it does all the behind-the-scenes calls.

 

AI Agents as Multi-Repo CRUD

 

These agents aren’t just chatbots that handle small talk. They’re designed to read, create, update, and delete records across various systems. So it’s no wonder some folks jumped straight to “We’ll never need multiple apps again!” But hold your horses. Doing CRUD in multiple repos is powerful, sure—but what about compliance checks, best practices, or specialized logic? That’s where SAP’s deep knowledge comes in. An agent that can manipulate data everywhere still needs the underlying rules that say how that data should be managed. If it was true, it would be like saying that the sole power of SAP S/4 is the DB it seats on… and that is not the case.

 

The Claim: Traditional Backends Replaced

 

Then there’s the big line everyone is quoting: “SaaS is dead.” Well, Software-as-a-Service is basically a commercial and deployment model. It’s not so much “dead” as it is evolving. Instead of you logging into ten different SaaS solutions, an AI agent might be the single entry point. Sounds neat—but those solutions’ logic layers don’t magically disappear in the background.

 

2 - Why ERP Backbones (Like SAP) Won’t Simply Disappear

 

The Misinterpretation of “SaaS is Dead”

 

Let’s address that hot phrase: “SaaS is dead.” By that logic, you might think we’d no longer pay for cloud services or need them. But what Nadella (and the general AI conversation) is saying is more about how we consume the apps, not that Agents and and DB is all you need.

 

Five Decades of IP

 

SAP has five decades of enterprise know-how: that’s five decades of perfecting how to close your books, manage taxes worldwide, handle supply chain complexities, comply with local laws, and more. That stuff is not “CRUD.” It’s not just reading and writing data. It’s knowledge that took years to refine and can’t be magically replaced by any AI agent—at least, not if you want to stay on the right side of the authorities!

 

Agents as API Consumers, Not Replacements

 

Realistically, AI agents will become top-tier API consumers. They’ll talk to SAP S/4HANA, they’ll talk to your CRM, they’ll pull data from your warehouse or data lake. But they’ll do it in a way that leverages the existing rules and validations from those apps. The agent will be “the best user ever,” never forgetting a step. But the core processes—like how to create a valid invoice or run MRP—still rely on the ERP’s capabilities.

 

3 - The Evolving Role of SAP in an Agentic World

 

From UI to “Skills” Integration

 

If you’ve been keeping an eye on SAP announcements, you’ve probably heard about SAP Joule, this digital assistant that’s supposed to make life easier by handling a bunch of SAP functionalities on behalf of the user. Joule steps in like a “digital copilot.” Instead of you navigating into three different SAP apps, Joule can do it all for you behind the scenes—firing off the right API calls while you chat or type in a single interface.

 

Now, this includes specialized flavors like “Joule for ABAP developers.” It’s a big deal if you’re a developer who lives and breathes ABAP because it promises to help you code better, enforce best practices, and hopefully push modern, “clean core” approaches. If you ask me, it’s about time we had something like this—ABAP is crucial for building or customizing many SAP solutions, and AI can help push code quality to new heights.

 

Innovation in Extensions and APIs

 

As we move more and more toward microservices, open APIs, and a composable ERP approach, SAP’s future with Joule and other AI-driven tools gets even brighter. The easier and clearer these APIs become, the more fluidly an AI agent can integrate. And for ABAP specifically, we’ll (hopefully) see fine-tuned models that deliver top-notch code snippets and design patterns, not the spaghetti code we sometimes see floating around online.

 

Agents: A Catalyst, Not a Conqueror

 

So, is ERP going to die? Absolutely not. AI agents are catalysts for ERP usage, not conquerors. They automate tasks, improve productivity, and reduce friction. But the heavy-lifting is still done by systems like SAP S/4HANA, guided by decades of best practices. The AI agent can simply orchestrate it all more elegantly—so end users can do what they do best without getting lost in transaction codes.

 

4 - Closing Thoughts: Joule’s API Integration Is SAP’s Next Big Leap

 

Here’s the bottom line: SAP might dominate the business application layer, but if it doesn’t fully expose that power in the new AI-driven UX layer, it risks falling behind heavy-hitters like Microsoft and Oracle. In my view, Joule can (and should) be the game-changer—yet its ultimate impact hinges on how robust its API integration is. The more “skills” Joule has, the better it can serve as a one-stop shop for enterprise tasks. And those skills will only exist if SAP invests in building and refining these APIs.

 

In other words, SAP must make it a top priority to give developers (and AI agents) easy access to all that rich business logic. It’s not enough to have 50 years of best practices locked behind a walled garden. We need to let Joule tap into that knowledge so it can handle real-world scenarios—everything from old-school ABAP to brand-new BTP-based designs.

 

If SAP nails this, it stands to solidify its role as the backbone for the next wave of GenAI-driven business transformation. If it doesn’t, well…there are other players who’d love to swoop in and fill the gap. I’m betting on SAP to rise to the occasion—but only if it keeps its eye on the ball and truly opens up the platform.

 

Great 2025 everyone, and keep an eye on those Joule updates 🙂

entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 4 季度版本
entry.content:
更快、更智能、更高效地构建

欢迎阅读我们2024年第四季度的“产品新功能”博客系列。本文为翻译文，原文链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition，原文作者JesseSukhbayar。

SAP Build 是一款业务应用程序开发和自动化解决方案，提供了一套全面的低代码、专业代码和生成式 AI 工具。本季度，我们很高兴为大家介绍许多新版本，其中包括 AI 驱动的功能，这些功能将彻底改变您的自动化和创新能力。让我们一起来看看吧！

以下内容将为您介绍以下产品的具体亮点：

  • SAP Build Process Automation

  • SAP Build Code

  • SAP Build Apps

  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build 流程自动化

创建和管理流程变体

SAP Build 流程自动化现在支持创建和管理流程变体。通过预定义的流程步骤和防护机制，流程专家和专业开发人员可以使用纯配置的方式为不同的用户群体建模不同的流程变体。通过定义变体条件和操作，工作流可以根据您的具体业务需求进行调整。借助此功能，您可以为各种场景管理定制化的工作流，而不会影响整体流程模板。

您可以在 SAP 帮助门户 中了解如何创建和管理流程变体。

在流程中通过 Actions 使用 RFC / BAPI

为了帮助您扩展 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 私有版和本地部署解决方案，您现在可以基于 RFC / BAPI 创建 Actions。通过利用 BAPI 节点库，您可以直接集成 RFC Actions，从而简化集成流程。您可以轻松搜索和浏览 RFC Actions，以找到工作流所需的必要功能，同时维护系统完整性并降低复杂性。

这一功能使您能够更高效地将 SAP S/4HANA 的功能集成到自动化流程中，从而提升业务操作的灵活性和效率。

SAP Build Code

移动开发工具包（MDK）中的生成式 AI 功能增强

移动开发工具包（MDK）的生成式 AI 功能现已增强，可以帮助您简化应用程序创建过程。开发人员可以：

  1. 生成应用程序逻辑：自动生成基于元数据的逻辑，减少手动编码工作量。

  2. 创建表单和工作流：在 AI 的协助下快速开发表单和工作流，加速开发进程。

  3. 构建强大的移动解决方案：利用 AI 减少手动操作，专注于交付具有影响力的解决方案。

通过这些功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建移动应用，同时将更多精力投入到业务价值的实现中。

了解更多详情，请阅读 @BirgitS 的博客 。

SAPUI5 开发：TypeScript 和 UI5 Linter

使用 Joule 进行 AI 驱动的 TypeScript 迁移

Joule 是集成到 SAP Build Code 中的 AI 助手，现在支持将 SAPUI5 应用程序从 JavaScript 无缝迁移到 TypeScript。TypeScript 提供了更高的开发效率，为构建 SAPUI5 应用程序提供了一种结构化且可扩展的方法。了解更多关于这一强大功能的信息，请阅读 @martinhaeuser 的博客 。

UI5 Linter v1.0 发布

SAP Build Code 推出了 UI5 Linter v1.0，这是一款旨在确保代码质量并与最佳实践保持一致的工具。此版本使开发人员能够编写更清晰、更易维护的 SAPUI5 代码，从而提升整体开发生命周期的效率。

通过这些新功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建高质量的 SAPUI5 应用程序，同时减少错误并提高代码的可维护性。

扩展 SAP Fiori 应用程序的全新体验

适用于 SAPUI5 项目的 Adaptation Editor

SAP Build Code 现在推出了 Adaptation Editor，这是一款用于扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的新工具。Adaptation Editor 取代了 SAPUI5 Visual Editor，并在 SAP Business Application Studio 中提供，为跨环境开发提供了直观且一致的体验。开发人员可以利用这一更新后的工具，以更高的性能简化 SAPUI5 扩展。详情请参阅 @OliverGraeff 的博客 。

适应项目与扩展项目

SAPUI5 的灵活性支持适应项目（Adaptation Projects）和扩展项目（Extension Projects），使开发人员、关键用户和最终用户能够对应用程序进行定制化修改。适应项目现已成为扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的战略性方法，提供了基于代码定制的高级工具。要了解这些项目类型的区别和优势，请参阅 这篇博客 。

通过这些新工具和方法，开发人员可以更灵活、高效地扩展和定制 SAPUI5 应用程序，满足多样化的业务需求。

SAP Build Apps

为 BTP 目标服务添加额外的 HTTP 请求

在将外部服务与 SAP Build Apps 集成时，您现在可以配置额外的 HTTP 请求头，从而获得更高的控制性和灵活性。这一功能增强了集成安全性和定制化能力。此外，新的流函数（flow function）支持使用 multipart/form-data 作为请求体类型上传二进制文件。

要了解更多详细信息，请参阅 HTTP Destination Request 流函数参考文档。

通过这些更新，开发人员可以更安全、更灵活地集成外部服务，并支持更复杂的文件上传场景。

全新的顶部导航栏

SAP Build Apps 现在提供了更新的工具栏和导航菜单，为用户带来更直观的体验。常用的功能（如“变量”）现在可以直接从工具栏访问。同时，部分标签名称进行了更新，例如：

  • “UI Canvas” 更名为 “用户界面”

  • “Data” 更名为 “集成”

此外，使用频率较低的选项（如“主题”和“导航”）被归类到 “应用设置” 中，以减少界面杂乱感。常用操作（如“预览”和“发布”）则被显著地放置在右上角，简化了版本管理和部署流程。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Dan_Wroblewski 的功能评测博客 。

通过这些改进，SAP Build Apps 的用户界面更加简洁高效，帮助开发人员更专注于应用程序的设计和开发。

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule 与 Work Zone 标准版的集成

Joule 现已集成到 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版 中，为工作区提供了 AI 驱动的强大功能。这一集成带来了以下优势：

  • 智能工作区推荐：提升工作效率。

  • 主动洞察：根据任务和优先级提供定制化建议。

  • 简化的工作流：改善协作并帮助用户更专注于关键任务。

通过 Joule 的 AI 能力，用户可以更高效地管理工作区，并获得个性化的支持。

要了解更多关于 Joule 如何改变您的工作区的示例，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的博客 。

引导式流程向导

引导式流程向导（Guided Process Wizard）结合了 SAP Build Work Zone 和 SAP Build 流程自动化，简化了创建结构化、分步工作流的过程。通过这一集成，您可以在流程自动化中设计引导式流程，并将其作为向导部署到 Work Zone 中。

您可以利用动态 UI 元素（如卡片和小部件）构建可定制的工作流，从而简化复杂的入职和发票处理等任务。引导式体验功能包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 高级版 和 SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone 中。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的 FAQ 博客。

 

了解更多

如需查看完整的新功能列表，请访问以下资源：

  • What's New Viewer（新功能查看器）

  • SAP 帮助门户

  • SAP Road Map Explorer（ SAP 路线图探索器）

为了及时了解最新公告和创新动态，请关注 SAP Builders Community，并与社区中的其他 SAP Builders 交流互动。

entry.title: New SAP Discovery Center Mission: Mastering SAP S/4HANA Extension with SAP Build
entry.content:
From the very beginning, SAP Build has been introduced as a unified low-code platform, enabling developers of all skill levels to create, extend, and automate business applications with drag-and-drop simplicity. Later, its capabilities were expanded with the integration of Java and JavaScript development, enhanced by AI features. And now the latest enhancements of SAP Build include ABAP development capabilities, advanced generative AI features, and deeper integration with SAP S/4HANA and other business applications.
 
SAP Build simplifies extension development and accelerates ERP modernization by seamlessly connecting all SAP development tools — ABAP, Java, JavaScript, and low-code — to enable effortless collaboration across diverse skill sets.
 
Start the new mission Mastering SAP S/4HANA Extension with SAP Build to try out the power of the Fusion Development experience provided by SAP Build.
 

Who is the mission made for?

 
No matter if you are a professional developer or a citizen developer with small development experience, this mission will guide you to the process of developing your extension application within a few hours.

What is it about?

 
The tutorial scenario is based on the following story:
 
Suzan, a medical technician at SBA Medical Clinic, noticed a small issue with one of the medical devices during her routine equipment checks. To address it quickly, she used the Service Hub Client mobile app to report the problem directly from her smartphone. The app, powered by SAP's Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), immediately logged the incident in a central system, ensuring all service details were captured in one place.
 

 

Alex, the service manager at Health Tech Devices, received the report through the Service Hub Admin interface. He quickly reviewed the issue, prioritized it, and assigned a technician to fix the problem. Using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming model (RAP) power in background, Alex is able to assign the nearest processor of the issue, seeing all available technicians on the map within a selected range.
 

 

What will I do?

 
The final solution consists of several services and applications driven by SAP BTP.
 
 
You will start development by creating and deploying a CAP application using SAP Build Code. This application will be the heart of the project, processing data for equipment maintenance issues. All the steps will be done using the power of artificial intelligence provided by Joule. You will design a data model, expose it with the services, implement some custom logic and deploy to the Cloud Foundry environment, including SAP HANA artifacts.
 
 
You will then create another background service using SAP BTP ABAP environment. This will provide the data for the available incident processors. The data includes some processor information and geo-location. The main data of a processor is assumed to come from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud system (Business Partner data). For simplicity, this data is uploaded only once. In real production scenarios, there should be a more comprehensive integration. To reproduce this scenario, you don't need an access to a real SAP S/4HANA Cloud system as you will use the SAP Business Accelerator Hub Sandbox. In addition, you will create a service using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming model to search for the processors within a given distance range using the power of the SAP HANA database geo-engine.
 
 
To access the data from your front-end applications, you will create SAP BTP destinations.
 
When all background services are available you will use SAP Build Apps to create 2 cross-platform applications. You will use all the power of SAP Build Apps hidden behind the drag-and-drop simplicity, including advanced components, complex (but still intuitive) formulas, REST-based integration for non-standard data requests, and so on.
 

How can I start?

 
Try out the mission in SAP Discovery Center for free. The content of the mission is tested on a regular basis and will be kept up to date by integrating the latest services and features.
 
Even though this mission involves numerous services and applications, you can complete all the steps from start to finish using the SAP BTP Trial Account.
 
You are interested in more end-to-end missions? Pick up one of them from SAP Discovery Center.
 
We are excited to hear your feedback about this mission. Please feel free share your thoughts in the comment section below or reach out to us via Discovery Center support tool.

entry.title: How to setup for joule in SAP BTP- BAS
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 
 
In this Blog, I would like to share my experience of How to setup SAP BTP with joule

What is an AI Code Generator? 

An Al code generator is a computer program incorporating artificial intelligence that helps engineers write code more swiftly and precisely. When you type code in real-time, it generates code based on prompts or suggests code for auto- completion. Al code writers like these can translate between different programming languages and write their programs. Additionally, they help with automatic documentation generation and speedy code snippet discovery. 

They apply AI algorithms to select the best code for a specific program, enabling developers to create a program quicker. 

Three ways AI code generation functions: 

  1. When a developer starts inputting code, AI will attempt to autocomplete the sentence. 
  1. An AI algorithm makes a suggestion centered on the developer's objectives when the developer leaves an input in natural language. 
  1. A developer communicates directly with AI, requesting it to develop a particular code or repair a bug. 

 

SAP Joule 

Joule is a new and exciting generative AI assistant that intelligently changes how we interact with SAP's systems and applications. Joule was developed with a focus on simplifying and streamlining tasks through various touchpoints. 

Joule is named after renowned 19th-century English brewer, physicist, and mathematician James Prescott Joule. He was famous for many things, such as the first law of thermodynamics, the Joule cycle, Joule expansion, Joule's first and second laws. 

Similar to other AI assistants, you'll be able to communicate with Joule using native languages and chat. You can ask it questions like: "What table is a purchase order in? How many sales orders were created last month? Has invoice 7XXXXXX been paid yet?" In response, Joule will intelligently provide you with accurate technical and business process related information.  

SAP Joule differentiates itself from Chat GPT based on the compelling fact that Joule is an SAP solution falling under SAP BTP's AI cloud. Therefore, Joule is an advantage for SAP users because it was built with SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP applications in mind. 

 

How to setup SAP BTP with Joule. 

Navigate to your Trial Account. 

  1. 1. Go to Boosters, search for SAP Build Code and click on start. 

 

 

  1. 2. You can see this screen, after booster executed successfully. 

Here click on the Navigate to subaccount. 

 

 

 

  1. => Go to Services --> Instances and Subscriptions. 

     => Make sure that You Have only one dev space in Business Application Studio. 

 

 

==>confirm, there is only one dev space in BAS. 

 

 

4.==> Coming back to instances and subscriptions click on SAP Build Code. 

 

 

    ==> click on create 

 

 

    ==> then click on Build an application -> SAP Build Code -> Full Stack Application 

    ==> Provide the project name, description, select new dev space and click on Create 

 

 

==> You can see the new dev space created in lobby as well as in BAS (Business application studio) dev spaces. 

*Lobby

 

 

*BAS (Business Application Studio) dev spaces. 

 

 

=> click on the project you have created in the lobby. 

 

 

=> you can see the below screen and the diamond icon is the one which launch the SAP Joule 

=>By entering the prompt in the input field we can get the solutions according to the prompt we give.
